title: Identifier Policy and Grouping-Key Registry
purpose: Define the shape, stability, and published metadata for identifiers used by the standard.
scope: This specification covers specification, architecture, work-item, verification, and requirement identifiers; domain and grouping tokens; terminal sequence rules for sequence-bearing identifiers; and the machine-readable grouping-key registry.
context: Stable identifiers are the backbone of traceability. The standard cannot answer coverage questions if requirement IDs and artifact IDs drift or collapse into file-name conventions.

supplemental_sections.heading: Identifier Shape Summary
supplemental_sections.content:
The following EBNF-style summary describes the canonical identifier families defined by this specification.

```text
UPPER_LETTER ::= ? ASCII A through Z ?
DIGIT        ::= ? 0 through 9 ?
UPPER_ALNUM  ::= UPPER_LETTER | DIGIT

DOMAIN       ::= UPPER_LETTER , { UPPER_ALNUM }
GROUPING     ::= UPPER_LETTER , { UPPER_ALNUM }
SEQUENCE     ::= DIGIT , DIGIT , DIGIT , DIGIT , { DIGIT }

SPEC_ID      ::= "SPEC-" , DOMAIN , { "-" , GROUPING }
ARC_ID       ::= "ARC-" , DOMAIN , { "-" , GROUPING } , "-" , SEQUENCE
WI_ID        ::= "WI-" , DOMAIN , { "-" , GROUPING } , "-" , SEQUENCE
VER_ID       ::= "VER-" , DOMAIN , { "-" , GROUPING } , "-" , SEQUENCE
REQ_ID       ::= "REQ-" , DOMAIN , { "-" , GROUPING } , "-" , SEQUENCE
```

supplemental_sections.heading: Draft Evolution Note
supplemental_sections.content: Earlier drafts used retired identifier `REQ-ID-0002` for a combined domain-and-grouping token rule. The current draft splits that retired combined rule into `REQ-ID-0016` through `REQ-ID-0019` so domain versus grouping and character-set versus leading-character constraints stay atomic.

requirements.title: Use approved artifact identifier prefixes
requirements.statement: Artifact documents MUST use `SPEC`, `ARC`, `WI`, or `VER` as applicable.
notes: Decision records are not part of the core standard today; repositories that add them can do so through an optional local extension.

requirements.title: Use the REQ prefix for requirement identifiers
requirements.statement: Requirement identifiers MUST use the `REQ` prefix.

requirements.title: Keep domain tokens uppercase alphanumeric
requirements.statement: A domain token MUST use only ASCII uppercase letters and digits.

requirements.title: Keep domain tokens letter-starting
requirements.statement: A domain token MUST start with an ASCII uppercase letter.

requirements.title: Allow stable optional grouping segments
requirements.statement: Identifiers MAY include zero or more grouping segments after the domain token.
notes: Grouping segments work best when they reflect stable structure rather than dates or workflow state.

requirements.title: Keep grouping tokens uppercase alphanumeric
requirements.statement: A grouping token MUST use only ASCII uppercase letters and digits.

requirements.title: Keep grouping tokens letter-starting
requirements.statement: A grouping token MUST start with an ASCII uppercase letter.

requirements.title: Keep terminal sequences numeric
requirements.statement: A terminal sequence token MUST use only decimal digits.

requirements.title: Keep terminal sequences at least four digits long
requirements.statement: A terminal sequence token MUST use at least four digits.

requirements.title: Use terminal-free specification identifiers
requirements.statement: Specification artifact identifiers MUST use `SPEC-<DOMAIN>(-<GROUPING>...)`.

requirements.title: Use sequence-bearing architecture identifiers
requirements.statement: Architecture artifact identifiers MUST use `ARC-<DOMAIN>(-<GROUPING>...)-<SEQUENCE>`.

requirements.title: Use sequence-bearing work-item identifiers
requirements.statement: Work-item artifact identifiers MUST use `WI-<DOMAIN>(-<GROUPING>...)-<SEQUENCE>`.

requirements.title: Use sequence-bearing verification identifiers
requirements.statement: Verification artifact identifiers MUST use `VER-<DOMAIN>(-<GROUPING>...)-<SEQUENCE>`.

requirements.title: Use sequence-bearing requirement identifiers
requirements.statement: Requirement identifiers MUST use `REQ-<DOMAIN>(-<GROUPING>...)-<SEQUENCE>`.

requirements.title: Preserve identifier stability
requirements.statement: An assigned identifier MUST remain stable.

requirements.title: Prevent identifier reuse
requirements.statement: An assigned identifier MUST NOT be reused for a different artifact or requirement.

requirements.title: Keep identifiers authoritative inside the document
requirements.statement: The identifier in canonical artifact fields or a requirement record MUST be authoritative.

requirements.title: Keep file names non-authoritative
requirements.statement: The file name MUST NOT replace the document or requirement identifier in traceability links.

requirements.title: Keep requirement IDs aligned with their specification context
requirements.statement: A requirement identifier SHOULD reuse the domain and grouping segments of the specification that contains it.
notes:
This keeps related clauses easy to find and trace, especially from generated
evidence snapshots and other repository tooling.

requirements.title: Publish machine-readable grouping metadata
requirements.statement: The reference package MUST publish a machine-readable grouping-key registry and identifier summary as JSON data such as `artifact-id-policy.json`.

requirements.title: Publish identifier family shapes in the catalog
requirements.statement: The authoritative identifier catalog MUST define the `SPEC-<DOMAIN>(-<GROUPING>...)`, `ARC-<DOMAIN>(-<GROUPING>...)-<SEQUENCE>`, `WI-<DOMAIN>(-<GROUPING>...)-<SEQUENCE>`, `VER-<DOMAIN>(-<GROUPING>...)-<SEQUENCE>`, and `REQ-<DOMAIN>(-<GROUPING>...)-<SEQUENCE>` shapes.
